title: Ladybug Graph Store
description: LadybugGraphStore implements the GraphStore contract using LadybugDB as the storage engine. It defines physical schema, persistence layout, full-text indexing behavior, and bulk-load mechanics.

rules: LadybugGraphStore shall implement the GraphStore contract using LadybugDB.
It acts as an infrastructure adapter identified by the backend id ladybug.
The adapter must handle database connection, schema DDL, version state, and operation translation.
LadybugGraphStore shall use configPath as the root for persistent graph and temporary scratch directories.
Database, lock, WAL, and scratch files shall live only under derived directories.
LadybugGraphStore shall implement recreate by discarding all persisted Ladybug graph data.
The backend must be ready to be opened again and rebuilt from scratch.
LadybugGraphStore shall own the Ladybug-specific persisted schema for files, symbols, specs, relations, and metadata.
The physical schema remains an implementation concern of the adapter.
The schema shall define node tables for File, Symbol, Spec, Document, and Meta.
The File table shall store content for deriving symbol snippets.
The schema shall define relationship tables for persisted relation types including EXTENDS, IMPLEMENTS, OVERRIDES, and coverage links.
COVERS_SYMBOL relations must preserve edge metadata.
LadybugGraphStore shall implement search using Ladybug's full-text search facilities.
It must prioritize exact identity matches and use OR logic for multi-token queries.
Search results must include match-aware snippets and 1-based line ranges.
LadybugGraphStore shall track a backend-specific schema version.
A destructive rebuild may be required if the schema version is incompatible.
LadybugGraphStore shall support efficient bulk loading.
Scratch files must be scoped to the current indexing run and cleaned up appropriately.
The backend shall own the concrete database files required by the implementation.
LadybugGraphStore shall persist store-level metadata needed by the abstract contract.
LadybugGraphStore shall use prepared statements for queries accepting user-supplied parameters.
Direct connection queries may be used for DDL statements and administrative queries.

constraints: Storage-agnostic consumers must depend on code-graph:graph-store instead of this spec.

scenarios.name: Backend initialization stays inside the adapter
then: the adapter initializes its specific connection and schema state
